artifacts.content:
Hi Ms. Alvarez,

I wanted to let you know what happened with Ethan this morning and why he’ll be out today and likely tomorrow (8/29–8/30).

Around 7:45 a.m., he was biking from our house at 712 North Phelps toward Lakemont, like he usually does. A white SUV came flying down North Phelps so fast trying to beat the light at Mizell that he panicked, swerved, and wiped out right in the street in front of our driveway. He wasn’t actually hit, but he hit the pavement hard enough that I didn’t feel comfortable just brushing him off and sending him on to school.

I ended up driving him straight to the AdventHealth Orlando ER to get him checked for a concussion and have X‑rays done on his wrist and shoulder. They think it’s mostly bruising and a mild concussion, but they told us to keep him home for at least two days to rest, limit screens, and watch for any changes. He’s pretty shaken up, and honestly so am I. This is just one more scary example of the speeding and cut‑through traffic we’ve been seeing on North Phelps between Alabama Drive and Mizell Avenue.

Could you please send over anything he’ll miss today and tomorrow so we can catch up over the weekend once he’s feeling better? Ethan is worried about falling behind in 5th grade already.

Thank you for understanding, and please email or call me at 407‑555‑2934 if you need any additional details.

Best,
Laura Henderson
712 North Phelps Avenue
Winter Park, FL 32789
laura.henderson712@gmail.com

Group text: “Phelps Neighbors”

Laura (712): Hey all, just a heads up about another scary one today. Around 5:20 p.m. Sophie ran out from behind my parked SUV in front of 712 to grab her backpack and a gray sedan coming from Mizell had to lock its brakes. It skidded several feet and stopped just short of her. I’m still shaking.

Greg (705): Oh my god, is she okay??

Laura (712): She’s okay, just scared. The driver got out and started yelling that he was late for his shift at the AdventHealth Winter Park ER and that “everyone uses this street to avoid Fairbanks.” That’s exactly the problem – it’s basically a cut‑through between Fairbanks and the medical stuff near Mizell now.

Michelle (718): This is insane. Between that and Ethan’s near‑crash on his way to Lakemont last month and my mailbox getting hit twice, it feels like we’re just waiting for something worse.

Evelyn (721): Don’t forget your mom almost getting knocked over crossing from your driveway to her car back in May, Laura. She told me she’s scared to come over between 4–6 p.m. now.

Laura (712): Yep. That pickup flying down from Alabama in early May and slamming on the brakes a few feet from her really shook her, especially after her stroke at AdventHealth Altamonte in February. She almost fell right in the middle of North Phelps.

Thomas (699): This is exactly why I avoid backing out during rush hour. I nearly got clipped backing out behind a speeding truck last November.

Laura (712): Same thing happened to Dan on his way to a late shift at Lockheed. That’s when we finally spent $280 on dashcams for both cars.

Michelle (718): We need real traffic calming between Alabama and Mizell, not just hoping people slow down.

Apple Notes
Title: Phelps speeding log – setup + thoughts

Created: 2024-01-10

I finally admitted to myself that the “mental tally” of scary cars on North Phelps isn’t cutting it. Since January 1, I’ve been keeping a proper spreadsheet (Google Sheet: “Phelps Speeding Log Jan–Mar 2024”) to track what we’re actually seeing between Alabama Drive and Mizell Avenue.

Structure:
- Date
- Time window (7:15–8:15 a.m. or 4:30–6:00 p.m.)
- Direction (toward Mizell / toward Alabama)
- Est. speed from the speed‑gun app (log anything over 35 mph; posted limit is 25)
- Plate #
- Vehicle/driver notes

Example entries so far:
• “Blue Honda, FL tag ABCD12, mom clearly headed to Lakemont Elementary car line, ~38 mph, 7:32 a.m., northbound toward Alabama.”
• “White contractor van, no logo, coming from AdventHealth Orlando campus entrance on Mizell, ~40 mph, 5:05 p.m., southbound.”

It feels a little obsessive, but we’ve had too many close calls: Ethan wiping out in front of 712 on August 29, 2024 when that white SUV flew by; Mom almost falling in early May 2024 crossing from our driveway to her car for PT after her February stroke at AdventHealth Altamonte; Sophie’s near‑miss on October 3, 2023 when the gray sedan skidded several feet and stopped just short of her.

Goal is to have at least a few months of consistent data so I’m not just saying “it feels fast” – I can point to specific times, speeds, and patterns (e.g., AdventHealth shifts, Lakemont drop‑off).

To: emily.morales84@gmail.com
From: laura.henderson712@gmail.com
Subject: Mom, kids, and this street 🙄
Date: 2024-05-06

Hey Em,

Catching my breath after another circus of an afternoon and wanted to fill you in on Mom and the general chaos on North Phelps.

First, Mom: she had her follow‑up PT at AdventHealth Altamonte today for the stroke she had back in February. She insisted on driving over here first, parking across from our place at 712, and then crossing the street to ride with me. I was standing in the driveway and watched her start to cross and my heart just dropped – a pickup came down the hill from Alabama Drive at what had to be close to 40 mph. He slammed on the brakes only a few feet from her. She froze and almost lost her balance. After everything with the stroke, she does NOT need that kind of adrenaline.

On the way to Altamonte she kept saying she doesn’t want to visit during the 4–6 p.m. window anymore because it feels like a racetrack. I can’t blame her. Between that, Sophie’s near‑miss back in October when the gray sedan from Mizell skidded to a stop right in front of her, and Ethan wiping out last August and ending up in the AdventHealth Orlando ER for a concussion check, I feel like I’m constantly braced for impact.

Otherwise, normal life: Ethan’s working on his science fair idea (something with plant growth and different light colors), Sophie’s soccer at Ward Park is in full swing, and Dan’s buried in a Lockheed deadline. Mom says she misses your Sunday calls; maybe you can FaceTime her this week?

Love you,
L

To: [Agent Name] <[agent]@statefarm.com>
From: laura.henderson712@gmail.com
Subject: Policy questions + dashcam receipts
Date: 2023-11-18

Hi [Agent Name],

I hope you’re doing well. I’m writing with a couple of questions about our homeowners and auto coverage after yet another close call on our street.

We live at 712 North Phelps Avenue in Winter Park, between Alabama Drive and Mizell Avenue. Over the last year or so, North Phelps has turned into a cut‑through for people trying to avoid Fairbanks and get to the AdventHealth Winter Park / Orlando campuses and the medical complex near Mizell. The posted speed limit is 25 mph, but it’s common to see cars going 35–40.

Last week, my husband Daniel was backing out of our driveway on his way to a late shift at Lockheed Martin when a speeding pickup nearly sideswiped him. The driver didn’t even slow down. That was the final straw, so we purchased and installed front‑and‑rear dashcam systems on both of our cars for a total of about $280.

I have a few questions:
1. Is there anything we should update on our homeowners policy to better protect us from potential hit‑and‑run damage (e.g., someone taking out our fence, mailbox, or a parked car in front of the house)?
2. Do you need copies of the dashcam receipts or installation info for our auto policies, or is that only relevant if we ever have to file a claim?
3. Given the number of near‑misses (including my son’s bike wipeout in August and my mom’s near‑fall crossing the street earlier this year), is there any additional coverage you’d recommend?

I know you’re on Aloma Avenue, so if it’s easier I’m happy to swing by the office one afternoon.

Thanks in advance,
Laura M. Henderson
407‑555‑2934

Event: Neighborhood walk + traffic observation
Date: 2024-09-05
Time: 4:30–6:00 p.m.
Location: North Phelps Avenue (Alabama Dr to Mizell Ave)
Attendees: Laura, Dan, kids (maybe), Michelle (718), Greg (705), Evelyn (721), Thomas (699)

Notes:
- Do our usual evening dog walk with Bailey but be intentional about watching traffic between Alabama Drive and Mizell Avenue.
- Bring phone with speed‑gun app + notebook to jot down a few examples for the Phelps Speeding Log spreadsheet (focus on anything over 35 mph; posted 25).
- Pay special attention near our driveway at 712 where:
  • Ethan wiped out on his bike on 8/29/24 when that white SUV flew past and we ended up at AdventHealth Orlando ER for concussion check/X‑rays.
  • Mom almost fell in early May 2024 crossing from our driveway to her car for PT at AdventHealth Altamonte when the pickup came down from Alabama and stopped only a few feet from her.
  • Sophie’s October 3, 2023 near‑miss with the gray sedan coming from Mizell that skidded several feet and stopped just short of her. Driver said he was late for his ER shift at AdventHealth Winter Park and “everyone uses this street to avoid Fairbanks.”
- Ask neighbors to share any new incidents: hit mailboxes, clipped mirrors, backing‑out close calls like Dan’s near‑sideswipe last November on his way to Lockheed.
- Try to note patterns: shift‑change surges from AdventHealth Orlando campus entrance on Mizell, Lakemont Elementary pick‑up traffic, etc.
- End at home by 6:00 so we can get dinner going and kids started on homework.

metadata.title: Neighborhood walk + traffic observation
metadata.location: North Phelps Avenue, Winter Park FL 32789

artifacts.content:
To: michelle.torres718@gmail.com; greg.wallace705@gmail.com; evelyn.carter721@gmail.com; thomas.nguyen699@gmail.com
From: laura.henderson712@gmail.com
Subject: Drafting our neighborhood traffic summary
Date: 2026-01-10

Hi all,

Finally sitting down at the kitchen table after getting Ethan through his science fair board and Sophie to bed, and I wanted to share some thoughts while they’re fresh.

Living at 712 North Phelps for the past several years, it’s so clear how much the traffic has changed between Alabama Drive and Mizell Avenue. Morning rush (7:15–8:15) and late afternoon (4:30–6:00) are the worst. Since January 2024 I’ve been keeping that spreadsheet where I log license plates, approximate speeds from the speed‑gun app, and notes like “blue Honda, FL tag ABCD12, mom in Lakemont Elementary car line” or “white contractor van coming from AdventHealth Orlando campus entrance on Mizell.” Anything over 35 mph goes in there, and there’s a lot.

We also have the personal stuff that keeps replaying in my head:
- Ethan’s bike wipeout on August 29, 2024 around 7:45 a.m., right in front of our driveway, when a white SUV sped past so fast he swerved and fell. We spent the morning at AdventHealth Orlando ER doing concussion checks and X‑rays and he missed two days of 5th grade.
- Mom’s scare in early May 2024, crossing from our driveway to her car parked across the street for her PT appointment at AdventHealth Altamonte. After her February stroke, having a pickup come down from Alabama at ~40 mph and stop only a few feet from her was beyond terrifying.
- Sophie’s near‑miss on October 3, 2023 around 5:20 p.m. when she darted from behind my SUV and that gray sedan from Mizell left several feet of skid marks and stopped just short of her. The driver saying he was late for his ER shift at AdventHealth Winter Park and that “everyone uses this street to avoid Fairbanks” sums it up.

Add in Dan’s near‑sideswipe backing out last November (cue the $280 dashcams) and your hit mailboxes and clipped mirrors, and it’s obvious this isn’t just a one‑off issue.

Happy to pull together a one‑page summary of these patterns and incidents if you all send me any dates/details I’ve missed.

Thanks,
Laura

Notes to self:

- Total Number Of Affected Properties Lots: 34.0
- Date Of Submittal Of Petition To Cwp: 2026-01-22
- One Email: dan.henderson712@gmail.com

- Two Signature Required: Michelle A. Torres
